claim_boundary: cuNxon GPU execution has runtime/dynamics viability and exposes snapshot/pattern/multi-sphere/interface APIs from Python, but most task-coupled probes remain diagnostic/negative. The Aigarth action route remains the only constructive action-quality lead so far, and even its target-contract/augmented-train variants stay baseline-level on low-margin stress_holdout. Qubic NIA Vol. 8 adds branching ratio/criticality as an explicit regime-diagnostic hypothesis. The branching-regime scan observed near-critical-looking proxy ratios beside baseline-level stress_holdout; direct avalanche-window probes made activation-transition estimates observable but did not by themselves produce robust task evidence. The task-coupled avalanche lane showed repeatable normal/hard holdout bumps, but stress_holdout remained at the best constant baseline. The controlled-regime calibration shows that low/medium/high drive scales move estimator/occupancy/entropy and action distributions, while stress_holdout still stays at 0.333333 and no drive regime beats the stress baseline. Criticality/decoder separation showed query/action collapse on low-margin execute/retry stress cases despite observable estimator movement. A new stress-injection upper-bound diagnostic deliberately put duplicated stress-like labels into Aigarth fitness, yet stress_train and stress_holdout still stayed at 0.333333 with 0/5 seeds beating constants. Criticality metrics and stress-label injection are useful diagnostics, not intelligence or generalization evidence. The stress geometry audit post-processes the live stress-injection artifact and shows both injected stress_train and original stress_holdout collapse to query on every sample; stress execute/retry accuracy is 0.000000 while augmented_train execute/retry remains 1.000000. This supports a low-margin separability/readout-geometry hypothesis, not an intelligence or generalization claim. The stress amplitude-ladder probe then shows the geometry hypothesis is actionable as separability evidence: original stress_holdout still stays 0.333333 with query-collapse 1.000000, but scaled stress_holdout reaches 0.833333 at 3.0x over fresh seeds 142..144. This is a label-injected/separability upper-bound, not intelligence or generalization evidence. The target-aligned stress objective follow-up preserves the 3.0x scaled-stress signal (scaled stress_holdout=0.888889, execute/retry=1.000000) but leaves original stress_holdout=0.333333, query-collapse=1.000000, and execute/retry=0.000000; this shifts the next question toward decoder/readout geometry, not longer runtime. The stress objective decoder/readout geometry follow-up is post-hoc: it confirms a 3.0x target-lane gain separates scaled stress while original stress remains at the constant baseline, so it narrows the bottleneck without improving task quality. The low-margin readout geometry probe makes that boundary explicit: original stress execute/retry lanes have a negative margin to the observed query boundary and remain at baseline, while 3.0x scaled stress crosses the boundary. This is a readout-geometry diagnostic, not intelligence evidence. The supervised low-margin target-objective follow-up optimized normalized low-margin train cases and reached perfect train/standard/hard holdout on this tiny route, but original stress_holdout remained 0.333333 with query-collapse 1.000000 and execute/retry accuracy 0.000000; counterfactual and permuted controls stayed 0.000000, so the evidence boundary remains negative for original stress/control generalization.

cunxon_aigarth_action_contract_penalty_p.evidence_boundary: five-seed heavy unexpected-label penalty audit; train-only Aigarth fitness; hard-holdout/permuted-control labels never optimized; no intelligence/generalization claim

cunxon_aigarth_action_hard_holdout_probe.evidence_boundary: five-seed hard/noisy holdout and permuted-control audit; train-only Aigarth fitness; no intelligence/generalization claim
cunxon_aigarth_action_hard_holdout_probe.verdict: hard-holdout audit keeps the Aigarth route promising but shows a large train-to-hard-holdout gap and only partial seed-level robustness

cunxon_aigarth_action_probe.evidence_boundary: small toy train/holdout Aigarth action slice; no broad intelligence/generalization claim
cunxon_aigarth_action_probe.verdict: Aigarth action probe beats constant-action baselines on this small train/holdout set, but remains a toy evidence slice

cunxon_aigarth_action_remap_audit.evidence_boundary: post-hoc replay over existing live cuNxon Aigarth artifacts; no new GPU learning run; isolates decoder vocabulary/remap effects only
cunxon_aigarth_action_remap_audit.verdict: signed-first-lane remap removes out-of-contract assertive labels, but remapped aggregate accuracy is slightly lower than the original decoder replay; decoder vocabulary was not the only bottleneck

cunxon_aigarth_action_seed_sweep.evidence_boundary: five-seed repeatability audit; small engineered train/holdout set; no intelligence/generalization claim
cunxon_aigarth_action_seed_sweep.verdict: Aigarth action seed sweep partially repeats the baseline-beating toy signal but remains brittle

cunxon_aigarth_action_strict_label_probe.evidence_boundary: five-seed strict-label hard/noisy holdout and permuted-control audit; train-only Aigarth fitness with unexpected-label penalty; no intelligence/generalization claim
cunxon_aigarth_action_strict_label_probe.verdict: strict-label fitness improved hard-holdout robustness but did not eliminate out-of-contract labels or brittleness

cunxon_aigarth_action_target_contract_au.evidence_boundary: five-seed augmented-train target-contract stress audit; train plus augmented_train Aigarth fitness only; hard/stress/control labels never optimized; no intelligence/generalization claim
cunxon_aigarth_action_target_contract_au.verdict: augmented low-margin training improves holdout/hard-holdout but stress-holdout remains baseline-level, so the target-contract route is still brittle

cunxon_aigarth_action_target_contract_pr.evidence_boundary: five-seed target-contract Aigarth fitness audit; signed-first-lane decoder used during train-only fitness; hard-holdout/permuted-control labels never optimized; no intelligence/generalization claim
cunxon_aigarth_action_target_contract_pr.verdict: target-contract fitness removes out-of-contract labels and improves holdout versus the post-hoc remap/heavy-penalty lanes, but remains brittle tiny Aigarth toy evidence with one hard-holdout baseline-level seed

cunxon_aigarth_action_target_contract_st.evidence_boundary: Deliberately optimizes stress-like labels as stress_train; negative upper-bound/debugging evidence because even injected low-margin stress cases stay at constant baseline. Not generalization, useful-computation, or intelligence evidence.
five-seed target-contract stress audit; target-contract train-only fitness reused, but stress_holdout and counterfactual_control labels were never optimized; no intelligence/generalization claim
cunxon_aigarth_action_target_contract_st.verdict: target-contract fitness keeps zero unexpected labels under harder stress/control splits, but stress_holdout falls to constant-baseline level and counterfactual_control is non-zero; this weakens broad generalization claims while keeping the route diagnostically useful

cunxon_aigarth_readout_probe.verdict: Aigarth can improve absolute output margins on the two-pattern fitness task, but this is not holdout/action evidence

cunxon_avalanche_intervention_seed_repli.evidence_boundary: fresh-seed replication of the task-coupled avalanche intervention lane; normal/hard holdout bumps repeated, but stress_holdout stayed at the best constant baseline and the branching/accuracy correlation was weakly negative; not intelligence evidence

cunxon_avalanche_intervention_task_corre.evidence_boundary: This is task-coupled regime instrumentation from bounded cuNxon snapshot windows; avalanche movement or branching-ratio estimates are not intelligence evidence unless held-out/stress task quality beats constant baselines under controls.

cunxon_avalanche_window_intervention_mat.evidence_boundary: This matrix tests whether the NIA Vol. 8 criticality/branching metric is stable under bounded window-length/sample-interval changes. It is not intelligence evidence: useful computation would require robust held-out/control task quality above baselines, not only movement in a snapshot estimator or one mode-specific bump.

cunxon_avalanche_window_probe.evidence_boundary: snapshot-level avalanche metrics from full-sphere states; diagnostics only because action accuracy remains at constant-action baselines
cunxon_avalanche_window_probe.verdict: Snapshot-level avalanche metrics are measurable, but neither infer nor train mode beats trivial baselines; criticality remains instrumentation rather than sufficient evidence.

cunxon_branching_regime_scan.evidence_boundary: coarse action-readout branching/activity proxy; near-critical-looking dynamics are diagnostic only and did not beat stress_holdout baselines

cunxon_controlled_regime_calibration.evidence_boundary: This is controlled-regime estimator calibration for Qubic NIA Vol. 8 style criticality diagnostics; drive-dependent branching or occupancy movement is not intelligence evidence unless stress/control task quality beats constant baselines.

cunxon_criticality_decoder_separation.evidence_boundary: This is post-hoc separation of estimator movement from decoder/action behavior over an existing live RTX 5090 calibration artifact. It uses no stress/control labels for optimization and is not intelligence evidence; useful-computation claims still require stress/control task quality above constant baselines across seeds and settings.

cunxon_external_drive_window_probe.verdict: input-class direct drive observable; hidden/output windows are not desired-output channels

cunxon_input_proxy_target_probe.verdict: input-proxy route confirms target drive but motor readout remains baseline-level

cunxon_interface_semantics_probe.verdict: absolute neuron-index readout semantics confirmed; relay behavior differs by source port class, not a learning result

cunxon_multisphere_action_probe.verdict: three-sphere adapter runs but is flat query and does not beat trivial baselines

cunxon_resident_action_probe.verdict: resident task-coupled action loop remains flat query and does not beat trivial baselines

cunxon_snapshot_pattern_probe.verdict: snapshot channels viable; pattern recall callable but flat zero in this setup

cunxon_source_semantics_audit.verdict: source-level semantics explain why output-port teacher forcing is unsupported

cunxon_stress_amplitude_ladder_probe.evidence_boundary: This is a label-injected separability upper-bound over scaled low-margin stress vectors. Scaled stress_train cases are inside the fitness callback, so any improvement is diagnostic adapter-capability evidence, not intelligence evidence and not generalization evidence unless stress/control holdouts beat constant baselines.

cunxon_stress_geometry_audit.verdict: Low-margin stress execute/retry vectors collapsed to neutral/query even when duplicated into fitness; run an amplitude ladder before adding more labels or longer runtime.

cunxon_stress_objective_decoder_geometry.evidence_boundary: Post-hoc decoder/readout geometry explains the latest stress-objective boundary but does not improve it: original low-margin stress_holdout still equals the best constant baseline, while the same cases become separable only after 3.0x scaling that was represented in the label-injected fitness path. This is not intelligence evidence and not generalization evidence.
cunxon_stress_objective_decoder_geometry.status: stress objective decoder geometry follow-up completed

cunxon_stress_objective_probe.evidence_boundary: objective-shaping diagnostic; original low-margin stress remains baseline-level, so not intelligence or generalization evidence

cunxon_supervised_low_margin_objective_p.status: aigarth target-contract supervised low-margin objective completed
cunxon_supervised_low_margin_objective_p.verdict: normalized low-margin train cases are learnable, but original stress_holdout remains at the constant baseline and controls remain below constants; objective-shaping diagnostic only

cunxon_supervised_motor_probe.verdict: supervised motor-target adapter remains flat query and does not beat trivial baselines

cunxon_vram_resident_run.verdict: four-hour resident process completed without observed GPU resource failure, but readout stayed flat zero and no decision-quality score was measured

qubic_nia_vol8_criticality_claim_map.evidence_boundary: hypothesis source only; supervised low-margin target objective is diagnostic and original stress/control task quality still does not beat baselines
qubic_nia_vol8_criticality_claim_map.summary: branching-ratio/criticality claims mapped to current artifacts; supervised low-margin objective completed and original stress/control remains baseline-or-below; no intelligence claim

verdict: Supervised low-margin target objective trains normalized low-margin cases but original stress/control remain baseline-or-below; not intelligence evidence
